div.p: Sir: The Department has received your dispatch No. 169, of the 9th instant, suggesting that a request be made by the minister at Madrid for the amelioration of the condition of Julio Sanguily, esq., during his continued confinement awaiting a new trial, and in reply you are informed that a copy was sent to Mr. Taylor on the 15th instant.

You are also informed that on the 13th instant a telegram was sent to the minister by the Department in the following words:
In view of Sanguily’s long confinement, now lasting nineteen months, and impairment of his health, you will ask all possible amelioration of his position pending retrial.

On the next day a telegram was received from Mr. Taylor stating:
Minister for foreign affairs promises all possible for Sanguily.

closer.signed:
Wm. Woodward Baldwin,
Third Assistant Secretary.
